title: A Super Hard Test for Smart Computers
summary: Scientists made a very hard test called Humanity's Last Exam to check how smart computers are. Before, computers did well on old tests, so scientists needed a new one. This test has 2,500 questions about math, science, history, and other tricky topics. Experts from around the world wrote the questions to make sure they are fair and have one right answer. They tested it on computers like GPT-4o and Claude, and most computers got low scores, showing the test is tough. The goal is to find what computers still can't do well, so people know their limits. A professor from Texas A&M helped make the test and said it's important to measure computer smarts correctly.

background_read: This article is about a new test for artificial intelligence (AI), which are computer programs that can think and learn. Scientists noticed that AI was getting too good at old tests, so they created a harder one called Humanity's Last Exam. It has many questions on different subjects to see where AI still struggles. Experts from all over worked together to make it fair. Testing showed that even advanced AI models had trouble, with low scores. This helps people understand what AI can and cannot do, which is important for using it safely and wisely in the future.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists and a professor from Texas A&M University
WHAT: Made a hard test called Humanity's Last Exam for computers
WHERE: Created by experts from around the world
WHY: To check what computers still can't do well and measure their smarts

perspectives.description: Scientists believe new tests are needed to accurately measure AI abilities and prevent overestimating what computers can do.
